name: Le Ponant
description: Le Ponant is the original ship of the Ponant fleet — a magnificent three-masted barque that launched the company in 1991 and established its identity as a purveyor of intimate, expedition-focused French luxury at sea. Carrying just 64 guests, Le Ponant offers one of the most exclusive and personal sailing experiences in the world, gliding silently under sail to destinations inaccessible to larger ships. In 2008 she was famously seized by Somali pirates while sailing without passengers; the crew was released safely within a week.

included: Open bar (house wines, spirits, soft drinks)

name: Le Boréal
description: Le Boréal ("The North Wind") is the first of Ponant's Boréal-class expedition ships, heralding the line's transformation from a single sailing yacht to a fleet of luxury expedition vessels. She carries 264 guests in 132 cabins and suites, almost all with private balconies, and is designed for immersive expedition cruising to remote destinations from the Arctic to Antarctica. Her elegant, contemporary French design by Jean-Philippe Nuel set the template for the entire class.

included: Open bar (wines, spirits, soft drinks, water, coffee)

name: L'Austral
description: L'Austral ("The South Wind") is the sister ship to Le Boréal, entering service in 2011. Like her sister, she carries 264 guests across 132 cabins and suites with a focus on expedition travel. L'Austral is particularly associated with Antarctic voyages and Patagonian itineraries, but also sails the Mediterranean, Northern Europe, and the Indian Ocean.

included: Open bar (wines, spirits, soft drinks, water, coffee)

name: Le Soléal
description: Le Soléal ("The Sun") is the third Boréal-class ship, entering service in 2012. In August 2013, her sister Le Soléal completed the Northwest Passage — becoming the first French commercial ship to traverse this legendary Arctic route. She sails varied itineraries including the Mediterranean, the Pacific islands, Asia, and polar regions.

included: Open bar (wines, spirits, soft drinks, water, coffee)

name: Le Lyrial
description: Le Lyrial is the fourth and final Boréal-class ship, delivered in 2015. Named after the lyre and the musical constellation, she is often chartered by private groups and has exclusive partnerships with institutions such as the Musée du Louvre. Le Lyrial offers the same immersive expedition experience as her three sisters, sailing itineraries across Europe, the Pacific, and polar regions.

included: Open bar (wines, spirits, soft drinks, water, coffee)

name: Le Lapérouse
description: Le Lapérouse is the first of Ponant's Explorer-class ships, named after the legendary French explorer Jean-François de Galaup, comte de Lapérouse. Entering service in 2018, she introduced a remarkable innovation: the Blue Eye — an extraordinary underwater lounge below the waterline featuring two large portholes and hydrophones, allowing guests to observe and listen to the ocean from within the ship. Her intimate size (184 guests) allows access to destinations larger ships cannot reach.

entertainment: Blue Eye (underwater lounge with ocean view portholes)
recreation: Expedition team of naturalists and scientists
included: Open bar (wines, spirits, soft drinks, water, coffee)

name: Le Champlain
description: Le Champlain is the second Explorer-class ship, named after the French explorer Samuel de Champlain, founder of Quebec City. Delivered in 2018, she shares the ground-breaking Blue Eye underwater lounge with her sister ships. Le Champlain regularly sails the Canadian Maritimes, the St. Lawrence Seaway, and the Northwest Passage — routes particularly suited to her name and heritage.

entertainment: Blue Eye (underwater lounge with ocean view portholes)
recreation: Expedition team of naturalists and scientists
included: Open bar (wines, spirits, soft drinks, water, coffee)

name: Le Bougainville
description: Le Bougainville is named after Louis-Antoine de Bougainville, the first Frenchman to circumnavigate the globe. Delivered in 2019, she is the third Explorer-class ship and like her sisters features the iconic Blue Eye underwater lounge. Bougainville was herself a botanist and explorer of the Pacific, and the ship bearing his name frequently sails Oceania, Antarctica, and the Mediterranean.

entertainment: Blue Eye (underwater lounge with ocean view portholes)
recreation: Expedition team of naturalists and scientists
included: Open bar (wines, spirits, soft drinks, water, coffee)

name: Le Dumont-d'Urville
description: Le Dumont-d'Urville is named after Jules Dumont d'Urville, the French naval officer who explored Antarctica and named Adélie Land after his wife. The fourth Explorer-class ship, delivered in 2019, she features the Blue Eye lounge and is often deployed on Antarctic voyages — fittingly, given her namesake's discoveries on that frozen continent.

entertainment: Blue Eye (underwater lounge with ocean view portholes)
recreation: Expedition team of naturalists and scientists
included: Open bar (wines, spirits, soft drinks, water, coffee)

name: Le Bellot
description: Le Bellot is named after Joseph René Bellot, the French naval officer who participated in Arctic expeditions searching for Sir John Franklin. The fifth Explorer-class ship, she entered service in 2020 and features the Blue Eye lounge. Bellot Strait — a narrow channel between Somerset Island and the Boothia Peninsula in Arctic Canada — bears his name, and the ship regularly sails those high Arctic waters.

entertainment: Blue Eye (underwater lounge with ocean view portholes)
recreation: Expedition team of naturalists and scientists
included: Open bar (wines, spirits, soft drinks, water, coffee)

name: Le Jacques Cartier
description: Le Jacques Cartier is the sixth and final Explorer-class ship, named after the Breton explorer who claimed Canada for France in the 16th century. Delivered in 2020, she completes Ponant's remarkable fleet expansion and features the iconic Blue Eye underwater lounge. Like her sisters, she carries just 184 guests in intimate luxury and deploys Zodiacs for shore landings in wilderness and remote coastal destinations.

entertainment: Blue Eye (underwater lounge with ocean view portholes)
recreation: Expedition team of naturalists and scientists
included: Open bar (wines, spirits, soft drinks, water, coffee)

name: Le Commandant Charcot
description: Le Commandant Charcot is Ponant's crown jewel and one of the most extraordinary ships ever built — the world's first hybrid-electric polar icebreaker designed for luxury cruising. Named after French polar scientist Jean-Baptiste Charcot, she is rated Polar Class 2, capable of breaking through Arctic and Antarctic ice to reach the Geographic North Pole and other destinations no other cruise ship can access. Powered by LNG and 5 MWh of battery storage, she can briefly sail in complete silence. Her onboard experience includes a Blue Eye underwater lounge, restaurants helmed by Michelin-starred chefs, and an immersive polar science programme.

dining: Hangar (casual à la carte restaurant, included)
entertainment: Blue Eye (underwater lounge — unique to Charcot)
Expedition lectures with scientists and guides
recreation: Nordic Spa (sauna and hot tub on open deck)
Expedition team of polar scientists and guides
Snowshoes, crampons and polar gear provided
included: Open bar (Champagne, wines, spirits, soft drinks, coffee)
